qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CRLF injection via user-controlled Location header in UE authentication endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a nil interface conversion panic in GetSupiFromSuciSupiMap (CWE-476) leading to DoS. This finding describes CRLF injection via unsanitized supiOrSuci concatenation into the Location header. Although both target the same endpoint (/nausf-auth/v1/ue-authentications) and same code file (ue_authentication.go), the root causes and vulnerability classes are fundamentally different: missing nil check vs missing output sanitization.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability: a nil interface conversion in GetSupiFromSuciSupiMap (CWE-476) causing a panic and DoS in the free5GC AUSF service. The crlf-injection-location-header finding targets the same endpoint but describes a different root cause (CRLF injection via unsanitized Location header concatenation).
evidence: Advisory root cause: GetSupiFromSuciSupiMap performs interface{} to *context.SuciSupiMap conversion without nil check, causing panic
crlf-injection-location-header root cause: supiOrSuci concatenated unsanitized into Location header enabling CRLF injection
